sections.heading: 1. Introduction
content: This document outlines the Know Your Customer (KYC) requirements and document verification mandates for clients engaging with Adymire Agency ("the Company," "We," or "Us").
KYC is a critical process that helps us:
• Verify the identity of our clients
• Comply with legal and regulatory requirements
By engaging our services, you agree to comply with these KYC requirements and provide requested documentation in a timely manner.

subSections.heading: 2.1 Why Kyc Is Required
content: • Confirm you are who you claim to be
• Meet legal obligations under Indian law
• Comply with tax regulations and reporting requirements
• Follow "Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002" (PMLA) guidelines
• Assess business risk associated with client relationships
• Maintain proper records for accounting and auditing
• Support tax filings and regulatory reporting

subSections.heading: 3.1 Mandatory Kyc
content: • All new clients before project commencement
• Existing clients for projects exceeding specified value thresholds
• Clients requiring official invoices with GST

subSections.heading: 3.2 Threshold Values
content: Enhanced KYC may be required when:

subSections.heading: 3.3 Exemptions
content: Simplified KYC may be acceptable for:
• Repeat clients with previously verified documents (within 2 years)
• Referrals from verified existing clients (Company discretion)
Note: The Company reserves the right to request full KYC regardless of exemptions if deemed necessary.

subSections.heading: 4.1 For Individual Clients (Indian)
content: • Recent utility bill (electricity, water, gas) - not older than 3 months
• Bank statement - not older than 3 months
• Cancelled cheque or bank account details

subSections.heading: 4.3 For Companies And Firms (Indian)
content: • PAN Card of the entity - MANDATORY
• Cancelled cheque or bank account details
• TAN (Tax Deduction Account Number) - if applicable

subSections.heading: 4.4 For International Clients
content: • Utility bill or Bank statement (not older than 3 months)
• Bank account details for wire transfer
Note: Documents must be in English or accompanied by certified translation.

subSections.heading: 5.1 How To Submit Documents
content: • Company will send an email requesting specific documents
• Email will include a checklist and submission instructions
• File formats: PDF, JPG, or PNG
• Include a cover note listing submitted documents

subSections.heading: 5.2 Document Quality Standards
content: • Clear and legible (no blurry or dark images)
• Colored scans preferred for government IDs

subSections.heading: 5.3 Verification Timeline
content: • KYC verification typically takes 2-5 business days
• Incomplete or unclear documents will be returned for resubmission
• Project work will commence only after KYC approval
• Urgent processing may be available on request (subject to availability)

subSections.heading: 6.1 Internal Verification
content: • Review all submitted documents for completeness and authenticity
• Check for consistency in name, address, and other details
• Validate PAN, GST, and other registration numbers (where applicable)

subSections.heading: 6.2 Third-Party Verification
content: • Video KYC for high-value projects (by appointment)

subSections.heading: 6.3 Re-Verification
content: • Every 2 years for ongoing client relationships
• When there are significant changes (address, ownership, etc.)
• For projects exceeding previously verified thresholds

subSections.heading: 7.1 Data Protection
content: • Documents stored in encrypted digital format
• Access restricted to authorized personnel only
• KYC information is kept strictly confidential
• Not shared with third parties except as required by law
• Used only for verification and compliance purposes

subSections.heading: 7.2 Data Retention
content: • KYC documents retained for 6 years from last transaction (as per PMLA)
• Secure deletion after retention period expires
• Clients can request document deletion subject to legal retention requirements

subSections.heading: 7.3 Data Breach Protocol
content: In case of any data breach:
• Affected clients will be notified immediately
• Steps will be taken to mitigate damage
• Incident will be reported to authorities as required

subSections.heading: 8.1 Incomplete Kyc
content: If KYC is incomplete or not submitted:
• Service agreements may be put on hold
• Company may decline to provide services

subSections.heading: 8.2 False Or Fraudulent Documents
content: Submission of false or fraudulent documents will result in:
• Legal action including filing FIR for fraud

subSections.heading: 8.3 Delayed Submission
content: • Delays in KYC submission will delay project timelines
• Company is not responsible for project delays due to pending KYC
• Deadline extensions may not be granted

subSections.heading: 10.1 When Video Kyc Is Required
content: Video KYC may be requested for:

subSections.heading: 10.2 Video Kyc Process
content: STEP 2: Join video call at scheduled time (Zoom, Google Meet, etc.)
STEP 3: Present original documents to camera
STEP 5: Company captures screenshots and records session (with consent)

sections.heading: 11. Kyc For Subscription Services
content: • Complete KYC required before first subscription activation
• Annual declaration confirming no change in details
• Immediate update if business details change
• KYC must be valid for auto-renewal to process
• Expired KYC will pause subscription until updated

subSections.heading: 12.1 When To Update
content: Clients must inform the Company and update KYC when:

subSections.heading: 12.2 Update Process
content: • Provide supporting documents for the change

sections.heading: 13. Refusal Of Services
content: The Company reserves the right to refuse services without providing reasons if:
• Documents are deemed suspicious or fraudulent
• Client is unable or unwilling to provide required documents
• Client appears on sanctions lists or watchlists

subSections.heading: 14.1 Reporting Obligations
content: The Company may be required to report to:
• Income Tax Department (for transactions above specified limits)
• Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) for suspicious transactions

subSections.heading: 14.2 Suspicious Transaction Reporting
content: • Company may file a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR)
• Client may not be informed to avoid tipping off
• Services may be suspended or terminated

sections.heading: 15. Client Rights
content: • Know what information is being collected and why
• Request copies of their submitted KYC documents
• Request correction of errors in KYC records
• Withdraw consent and discontinue services (subject to contractual obligations)

sections.heading: 16. Frequently Asked Questions (Faq)
content: Q1: Why is PAN Card mandatory?
A: PAN is required for tax compliance and is the primary tax identification in India.
A: Clear scanned copies are acceptable. Original documents may be verified in person for high-value projects.
Q3: What if I don't have GST registration?
A: GST registration is required only if your turnover exceeds the threshold. Otherwise, you can declare that you are not GST registered.
A: Aadhaar is not mandatory but is preferred as address proof.
Q5: How long does KYC verification take?
A: Typically 2-5 business days. Urgent processing available on request.
Q6: Can someone else submit documents on my behalf?
A: For individuals, no. For businesses, authorized signatories can submit with proper authorization letter.
Q7: What if my documents are in a regional language?
A: Documents in Hindi are generally acceptable. Other regional languages may require certified translation.

sections.heading: 17. Contact Information
content: Response time: 24-48 business hours for KYC queries

sections.heading: 18. Changes To Kyc Policy
content: The Company reserves the right to update KYC requirements based on:
Updated policies will be posted on our website and communicated to affected clients.

sections.heading: 19. Acknowledgment And Consent
content: By engaging our services and submitting KYC documents, you:
• Acknowledge that you have read and understood these KYC Mandates
• Consent to the collection, verification, and storage of your information
• Confirm that all submitted documents are genuine and accurate
• Understand the consequences of providing false information
• Agree to update information when changes occur
• Authorize the Company to conduct necessary verification checks
Failure to comply with KYC requirements may result in denial or termination of services.
